userStory: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with my credentials so that I can access my authenticated account experience and the additional features available after login.
This application is a single page application.
The Login page is not directly accessed by URL.
The user begins on the Home page at: http://localhost/washtabui/home The Login page/view is opened by clicking the person/profile button on the Home page.
After successful authentication, the application returns to the Home page in an authenticated state.
Ensure the Home page loads successfully in guest state.
Open the Login page by clicking the person/profile button.

The following valid credentials should be used for successful authentication scenarios: Valid Username : krisadmin Valid Password : Carwash#1 These valid credentials should be treated as approved test data for positive login scenarios in this workflow.

The Login page contains: Username field Password field Login button Password masking behavior Back button Home button These controls should be modeled as in the Login page POM/resource where appropriate, while allowing shared shell navigation controls to be reused from the upstream Home page resource when they are already owned there.

behaviorRules: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with my credentials so that I can access my authenticated account experience and the additional features available after login.
After successful authentication, the application returns to the Home page in an authenticated state.

The following valid credentials should be used for successful authentication scenarios: Valid Username : krisadmin Valid Password : Carwash#1 These valid credentials should be treated as approved test data for positive login scenarios in this workflow.

The Login page contains: Username field Password field Login button Password masking behavior Back button Home button These controls should be modeled as in the Login page POM/resource where appropriate, while allowing shared shell navigation controls to be reused from the upstream Home page resource when they are already owned there.
The Login page should open only through the Home page person/profile button in this workflow.
Username and Password are required fields unless business behavior states otherwise.
Password input should be masked by default.
Valid credentials should authenticate the user successfully.
For positive authentication scenarios, the workflow should use the approved valid credentials: Username : krisadmin Password : Carwash#1 Invalid credentials should not authenticate the user.
Failed login should keep the user on the Login page or in the unauthenticated state, with the expected error feedback displayed.
Successful login should transition the application back to the Home page in authenticated state.
Leading and trailing spaces in entered values should be handled according to expected business behavior and must be validated.

journey.action: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with my credentials so that I can access my authenticated account experience and the additional features available after login.
The Login page is not directly accessed by URL.
The user begins on the Home page at
The Login page/view is opened by clicking the person/profile button on the Home page.
After successful authentication, the application returns to the Home page in an authenticated state.
Ensure the Home page loads successfully in guest state.
Open the Login page by clicking the person/profile button.

keywordNames: Wait For Element To Be Ready
Open Home Page And Click User Button

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Login page opens from Home page using the person/profile button
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Login page/view opens from the Home page and displays Username field, Password field, Login button, Back button, and Home button in an interactive state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Login page controls are visible and enabled after navigation from Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Username field, Password field, Login button, Back button, and Home button are visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Login page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify password field masks entered characters by default
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Password field masks all entered characters and does not display the raw password text on screen.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify successful login using approved valid credentials returns user to authenticated Home state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application authenticates the user successfully, returns to the Home page in authenticated state, replaces the person/profile button with the logged-in user name, and displays authenticated-only features.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify invalid username prevents authentication and displays login error
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, an observable login error message is displayed, the application remains unauthenticated, and the user stays on the Login page or in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify invalid password prevents authentication and displays login error
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, an observable login error message is displayed, the application remains unauthenticated, and the Login page remains active.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify login attempt with both invalid username and invalid password is rejected
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication is rejected, visible error feedback is displayed, and the user is not redirected to authenticated Home state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify required field validation when Login button is clicked with both fields empty
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Required field validation is displayed for Username and Password fields and authentication does not proceed.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Username required validation when Password is entered and Username is blank
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: A visible Username required validation message is displayed and login does not proceed.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Password required validation when Username is entered and Password is blank
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: A visible Password required validation message is displayed and login does not proceed.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button on Login page returns user to Home page guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns to the Home page in guest state and the Login page is no longer displayed.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button on Login page returns user to Home page guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns to the Home page in guest state and the Login page is closed.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify leading and trailing spaces in valid username and password are handled during login
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application handles leading and trailing spaces according to business behavior, with either successful authentication after trimming valid values or visible rejection feedback while remaining unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify whitespace-only Username and Password values are not accepted
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Whitespace-only values are rejected, required or invalid input validation is displayed, and the application does not authenticate the user.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify special characters in invalid credentials do not bypass authentication
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The invalid credential values are rejected, authentication does not occur, and an observable login failure message is displayed.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify long Username and Password input values are handled without application crash
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Login page remains stable, authentication is rejected for invalid long credentials, and visible feedback or validation is displayed without UI corruption.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify user remains in guest state after failed authentication attempt
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application remains in guest state, authenticated-user UI is not displayed, and the person/profile button remains visible instead of a logged-in user name.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify authenticated Home page displays logged-in user identity after successful login
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The authenticated Home page displays the logged-in user name in place of the person/profile button and exposes authenticated-only functionality.

validations: Username field, Password field, Login button, Back button, and Home button are visible, enabled, and available for interaction on the Login page.
Authentication fails, an observable login error message is displayed, the application remains unauthenticated, and the user stays on the Login page or in guest state.
Authentication fails, an observable login error message is displayed, the application remains unauthenticated, and the Login page remains active.
Authentication is rejected, visible error feedback is displayed, and the user is not redirected to authenticated Home state.
Required field validation is displayed for Username and Password fields and authentication does not proceed.
A visible Username required validation message is displayed and login does not proceed.
A visible Password required validation message is displayed and login does not proceed.

The application handles leading and trailing spaces according to business behavior, with either successful authentication after trimming valid values or visible rejection feedback while remaining unauthenticated.
Whitespace-only values are rejected, required or invalid input validation is displayed, and the application does not authenticate the user.
The Login page remains stable, authentication is rejected for invalid long credentials, and visible feedback or validation is displayed without UI corruption.
The application remains in guest state, authenticated-user UI is not displayed, and the person/profile button remains visible instead of a logged-in user name.
transitions: The Login page/view opens from the Home page and displays Username field, Password field, Login button, Back button, and Home button in an interactive state.

The application authenticates the user successfully, returns to the Home page in authenticated state, replaces the person/profile button with the logged-in user name, and displays authenticated-only features.
Authentication fails, an observable login error message is displayed, the application remains unauthenticated, and the user stays on the Login page or in guest state.
Authentication fails, an observable login error message is displayed, the application remains unauthenticated, and the Login page remains active.
Authentication is rejected, visible error feedback is displayed, and the user is not redirected to authenticated Home state.
The application returns to the Home page in guest state and the Login page is no longer displayed.
The application returns to the Home page in guest state and the Login page is closed.

The application handles leading and trailing spaces according to business behavior, with either successful authentication after trimming valid values or visible rejection feedback while remaining unauthenticated.
The application remains in guest state, authenticated-user UI is not displayed, and the person/profile button remains visible instead of a logged-in user name.
The authenticated Home page displays the logged-in user name in place of the person/profile button and exposes authenticated-only functionality.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.
